findings.problem: The chapter's return beat lands as atmosphere and recognition, but not as a fresh operative turn. After the confession and the sex scene, the ending mostly restates that nothing is solved, so chapter_08 hands its real terminal consequence to chapter_09 instead of closing on a new action, demand, or choice inside the family.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the closing span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:549, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:553, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:555, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:557 so the dawn return changes leverage before the cut. Options: have Marcia ask one concrete question, have Delroy drop the sleep performance, or have Tiana choose explicitly what to conceal or disclose. Keep the ordinary-dawn texture, but end on a new pressure point rather than only on unsolved status.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised closing lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:553-559 contain an explicit new action, demand, or decision that changes what Tiana, Marcia, or Delroy must do next that morning; fail if the ending still stops at recognition plus summary of unresolved facts.

findings.problem: The second half revisits the same leverage question three times: is Tiana with Malik because she wants him, or because she needs an exit from home? The bus exchange, the pre-sex consent check, and the post-sex aftermath all restage materially similar ground, which blunts escalation and makes the chapter feel less shaped than it should at this level.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the work of the beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:285, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:381, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:479. Let the bus scene settle motive, let the line around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:381 handle present-tense consent only, and let the aftermath around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:479 move to consequence or future leverage instead of re-asking the same question. Compress or cut one restatement if necessary.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, when read in order, the bus exchange, the consent check, and the post-sex talk each do distinct work: motive is clarified once, consent is confirmed once, and the aftermath introduces a new consequence rather than reprising the same escape-versus-desire dilemma. Fail if all three exchanges still restate the same core question in similar terms.

findings.problem: The bus exchange briefly slips into transcript cadence. Tiana answers Malik with low-information assent three times in close succession ("Yes." / "I know." / "I know."), which violates the style-bible preference against multiple low-information replies and momentarily flattens her otherwise sharp, pressure-reactive voice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:315, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:323 so Tiana's replies do more than acknowledge Malik's read. Keep her exhaustion, but make at least one answer sharper, more self-exposing, or more defensive in her own register rather than another assent.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:313-323 contains no more than one consecutive low-information assent from Tiana and at least one of her replies materially shifts the argument. Fail if the exchange still reads as Malik diagnosing while Tiana only confirms.

findings.problem: Two prose scaffolds repeat past the point of choice and become visible tics: the chapter uses the 'She thought of...' setup three times and the 'The question...' meta-frame three times. In a chapter this physically specific, those repeated sentence shapes start to feel like authorial placeholders rather than fresh perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:129, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:303, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:99, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:471 so more of them register through scene-specific sensation, memory texture, or physical action instead of the repeated scaffolds. Do not announce what the question did if the next beat can embody the effect directly.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision, neither the 'She thought of...' scaffold nor the 'The question...' scaffold appears more than twice in chapter_08, and each cited beat reads as a distinct sensory or relational turn rather than the same sentence template. Fail if either scaffold still governs three or more key turns.

summary: FAIL. The chapter holds continuity, past-tense control, character-voice separation, and anti-sanitization standards: no continuity contradiction, tense drift, example-line reuse, fade-to-black intimacy, or sanitization drift was found. It still falls short of pass-level because the ending defers its sharpest family consequence, the Malik/Tiana leverage negotiation is restaged too many times, one bus exchange drops into low-information transcript cadence, and repeated prose scaffolds become visible tics.
